title: Sarcoglottis assurgens
taxonomy: Sarcoglottis assurgens is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Sarcoglottis assurgens is classified within the family Orchidaceae and the subfamily Orchidoideae. It is further categorized under the tribe Cranichideae. Within the tribe, Sarcoglottis assurgens is placed in the subtribe Spiranthinae. The precise classification of Sarcoglottis assurgens is further defined by its botanical name.

intro: Sarcoglottis assurgens, also known as "Guayabita" or "Pitahaya", is a flowering plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Orchidaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Sarcoglottis assurgens include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in the production of natural remedies for skin care and wound healing.
